channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Evolution of Gastrointestinal Inflammatory Diseases and Neoplasm Burden in Super-Elderly Populations: Integrated GBD 2023, CHARLS, and CLHLS Analyses of China and G20 Countries.
item.description: Molecular medicine (Cambridge, Mass.) - Rapid population aging has profoundly altered the epidemiological profile of gastrointestinal diseases in individuals aged ≥75 years. Although enteric infections (EI) have been effectively controll...

item.title: Structure-based machine learning model for discovering pregnane X receptor (PXR) agonists and biological activity validation.

item.title: Healthcare Providers' Perspectives on the Role of Artificial Intelligence in the Care of Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease: An International Survey.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) care, but concerns over data quality, privacy, and provider familiarity remain. To examine international health care pr...

item.title: Association between circulating metabolic biomarkers and the risk of inflammatory bowel disease: a prospective cohort study.
item.description: International journal of colorectal disease - Letter -

item.title: Global research trends in ferroptosis in autoimmune diseases: a bibliometric and visual analysis (2018-2025).

item.title: Matrix metalloproteinases in intestinal fibrosis and fibrostenosing Crohn's disease: Current evidence, mechanistic hypotheses, and translational challenges.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Fibrostenosing complications in Crohn's disease arise from mixed transmural remodeling that includes inflammation, fibrosis, and muscular hypertrophy/hyperplasia. Matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs) a...

item.title: Pathological reprogramming of innate immune cells drives resistance to biologics in inflammatory bowel diseases.
item.description: Autoimmunity reviews - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), encompassing Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is a chronic immune-mediated disorder characterized by persist ent intestinal inflammation. With a r...

item.title: Rare case report and literature review of peripheral T-cell lymphoma presenting as massive gastrointestinal hemorrhage: an unusual etiology demanding emergency surgical intervention.
item.description: Frontiers in oncology - Case report - We report a case of peripheral T-cell lymphoma (PTCL) initially presenting as idiopathic recurrent gastrointestinal hemorrhage, which had previously been misdiagnosed as multiple gastrointestinal u...

item.title: Chishizhi Yuyuliang Decoction Ameliorates Ulcerative Colitis in Mice by Promoting Colonic Epithelial Mucosal Healing via the CAV1/FA Axis.
item.description: Rapid communications in mass spectrometry : RCM - This study aimed to investigate the therapeutic mechanisms of CY in treating UC using an integrated metabolomics and proteomics approach. A murine UC model was induced using dextran sulfate sodium ...

item.title: Association Between Toothbrushing Habits and Mucosal Healing in Japanese Patients with Ulcerative Colitis.

item.title: EFFICACY OF WATER INFUSION VERSUS AIR/CO2 INSUFFLATION FOR COLONOSCOPY: A SYSTEMATIC REVIEW AND META-ANALYSIS.
item.description: Arquivos de gastroenterologia - Meta-analysis - Colonoscopy is a widely used screening method for colorectal cancer, playing a crucial role in early detection and prevention by allowing visualization and removal of precancerous lesions. It also ...

item.title: The Prevalence of Fibromyalgia in the General Population and At-Risk Subpopulations: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Anesthesia and analgesia - Estimates of fibromyalgia prevalence vary widely worldwide due to differences in diagnostic criteria, study methodology, and population characteristics. Accurate estimates are essential to inform p...

item.title: Berberine Improved the Therapeutic Efficacy of UC-MSCs for DSS-Induced Colitis via Aerobic Glycolysis.
item.description: Phytotherapy research : PTR - The global prevalence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) continues increasing, but effective targeted treatments remain elusive. Recently, mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) have emerged as a promising...

item.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease Is Associated With Persistent Functional Impairment and Increased Short- and Long-Term Complications Following Total Knee Arthroplasty.
item.description: Orthopedic reviews - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is increasingly prevalent, and more affected patients are undergoing total knee arthroplasty (TKA). However, the effect of comorbid IBD on short- and long-term post...

item.title: Choosing NSAID treatment in axial spondyloarthritis: factors guiding clinical decision-making.
item.description: Current opinion in rheumatology - Review - Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) are first-line therapy for axial spondyloarthritis (axSpA), improving pain, function, and disease activity. This review evaluates evidence for NSAID ef...

item.title: Communicating Risk and Safety of Inflammatory Bowel Disease Therapies.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) often require long-term treatment with advanced therapies, including biologics and small molecules. The use of these agents must be carefully balanced against the ...

item.title: Anti-Integrin αvβ6 Autoantibodies as Diagnostic and Monitoring Biomarkers for Pediatric-Onset Primary Sclerosing Cholangitis.
item.description: Hepatology research : the official journal of the Japan Society of Hepatology - Pediatric primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is a rare progressive cholestatic liver disease that often presents diagnostic challenges owing to overlapping clinical and histological features with...

item.title: Gastrointestinal-specific Anxiety in IBD: Associations with Disease Activity, Psychological Symptoms, and Serotonergic Polymorphisms.
item.description: Biopsychosocial science and medicine - Many people living with an inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) experience persistent abdominal symptoms even in the absence of active inflammation. These symptoms are referred to as irritable bowel sy...

item.title: Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor Pathway Is a Potential Biological Mechanism in Inflammatory Bowel Disease-Induced Psoriasis.
item.description: Journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - Psoriasis and inflammatory bowel disease are characterized by relapsing episodes of immune-mediated, chronic inflammation and frequently co-occur. However, the potential causal relationship between...

item.title: Lonely minds, inflamed guts: metabolic and circulating protein pathways linking social isolation and loneliness to inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Translational psychiatry - Mounting evidence suggests that psychosocial stressors, such as social isolation and loneliness, contribute to gastrointestinal disorders by disrupting gut-brain interactions. We aim to investigate...
